Title: E2E_ClaimSearch_PortalToDatabase

Title: E2E_ClaimAdjudicationBatch_TWS
Step Action: Trigger the Claims Submission TWS job via the Job Execution Tier (TWS console or scheduled trigger)
Step Expected Result: TWS job is accepted and transitions to Running state in the TWS console

Step Action: Confirm the SSIS Server executes the associated SSIS package and reads pending claims from the SQL database
Step Expected Result: SSIS package log shows rows read from the pending claims queue in the SQL database

Step Action: Monitor job progress until the job reaches a Completed status
Step Expected Result: TWS job shows Completed with no errors or warnings in the job log

Step Action: Verify the claim records in the SQL database have been updated with adjudication results (status changed from Pending to Processed or Paid)
Step Expected Result: SQL query shows claim status updated for all claims processed in the batch run

Step Action: Confirm the processed claim status is visible via the Provider Portal claim search
Step Expected Result: Portal claim search returns the updated claim status matching the SQL record

Title: E2E_BizTalkBatchDomain_DataFlow
Step Action: Confirm a pending batch transaction exists in the App Batch Domain queue for the target tenant
Step Expected Result: App Batch Domain shows one or more pending transactions for the test tenant

Step Action: Trigger the BizTalk batch job for the App Batch Domain and monitor execution
Step Expected Result: BizTalk job starts and processes the batch transaction without errors

Step Action: Verify the BizTalk job writes the output to the Repository / CDM
Step Expected Result: Repository or CDM contains the new record created by the BizTalk batch job

Step Action: Query the Repository / CDM to confirm the data matches the input batch transaction
Step Expected Result: Record fields in Repository / CDM match the fields from the original batch transaction

Step Action: Confirm that no duplicate records are created if the job is run a second time with the same input
Step Expected Result: Second run detects the existing record and does not insert a duplicate

Title: E2E_BizTalkRuntimeDomain_DataFlow
Step Action: Submit a real-time transaction to the App Runtime Domain endpoint for the target tenant
Step Expected Result: App Runtime Domain acknowledges the transaction and returns a synchronous or acknowledgement response

Step Action: Monitor the BizTalk runtime pipeline for the transaction and confirm it enters the correct receive port
Step Expected Result: BizTalk admin console shows the message received on the correct receive port for the tenant

Step Action: Confirm the BizTalk orchestration or send port processes the message and routes the output
Step Expected Result: Orchestration log or send port log shows message processed and output sent to the correct destination

Step Action: Verify the result of the real-time transaction is stored or forwarded as expected by the integration design
Step Expected Result: Destination system (CDM / File Share / endpoint) shows the processed result

Step Action: Confirm the transaction is logged with the correct tenant ID and timestamp
Step Expected Result: BizTalk tracking database contains the transaction record with correct tenant and timestamp

Title: E2E_DataPipeline_DataTierToNextGen
Step Action: Insert a test record into the Windward Data Tier database (via a controlled test claim or member update)
Step Expected Result: Test record exists in the SQL database with a unique identifier for tracking

Step Action: Confirm the DataHub or on-prem service picks up the change and forwards it to the NextGen Staging endpoint
Step Expected Result: DataHub log shows the change event routed to the Staging endpoint

Step Action: Verify the test record appears in the NextGen Staging layer with correct values
Step Expected Result: Staging query returns the test record with all fields matching the source SQL record

Step Action: Confirm the record is promoted from Staging to the History / Query layer within the expected time
Step Expected Result: History / Query endpoint returns the test record when queried by its unique identifier

Step Action: Verify that the portal or reporting layer can read the data from History / Query
Step Expected Result: Provider Portal or reporting query against History / Query returns the test record accurately

Title: E2E_BIReporting_DataToRender
Step Action: Update a test record in the SQL Data Tier that feeds the BI State Tier (e.g. update a claim amount or member record)
Step Expected Result: SQL update is committed and the change is visible in the source table

Step Action: Confirm the BI State Tier data pipeline picks up the change within the documented refresh window
Step Expected Result: BI State Tier data store shows the updated value after the scheduled or triggered refresh

Step Action: Open the Power BI report for the target tenant and refresh the dataset
Step Expected Result: Power BI dataset refresh completes without errors

Step Action: Verify the report reflects the updated data from the SQL source
Step Expected Result: Power BI report shows the updated claim amount or member field matching the SQL change

Step Action: Confirm no cross-tenant data is visible in the report (tenant isolation)
Step Expected Result: Filtering the report by a different tenant returns no data for the test tenant's records

Title: E2E_IntegrationAPI_ExternalRequestToData
Step Action: Send a test HTTP request to the Integration API endpoint for the target tenant with a valid payload
Step Expected Result: Integration API returns HTTP 200 and a valid response body

Step Action: Confirm the Integration API processes the request and routes it through the internal pipeline
Step Expected Result: Integration API log shows the request received and forwarded to the appropriate internal service

Step Action: Verify the data from the request is written to the correct target (Data Tier SQL or Repository / CDM)
Step Expected Result: Target data store contains a new or updated record matching the Integration API request payload

Step Action: Query the target data store to confirm field-level accuracy
Step Expected Result: All fields in the target record match the corresponding fields in the Integration API request payload

Step Action: Confirm the Integration API test suite (Integration API Tests component) passes all assertions for this flow
Step Expected Result: Integration API test suite run returns 0 failures for the end-to-end flow scenario

Title: E2E_ServerToServer_WW1WebToApp
Step Action: Send a test request to the Windward Web Tier ALB endpoint and confirm it reaches the Web server
Step Expected Result: ALB access log shows the inbound request received on the correct listener

Step Action: Confirm the Web server processes the request and calls the App Tier via the NLB
Step Expected Result: Web server application log shows an outbound call to the NLB endpoint; NLB routes to an App server

Step Action: Verify the App server receives the request and queries the SQL primary database
Step Expected Result: App server log shows the database query executed against the correct SQL listener

Step Action: Confirm the SQL database returns results and the App server passes them back through the NLB to the Web server
Step Expected Result: Response chain is complete; Web server receives data from App server

Step Action: Verify the Web server returns the final HTTP response to the originating ALB client with the correct payload
Step Expected Result: ALB response log shows HTTP 200; response body contains the expected data payload
